Over the coastal waters and along the immediate coast, sustained winds were as high as 37 knots (43 mph) at the Miami Beach handar station (KMBF) and as high as 41 knots (47 mph) at Fowey Rocks C-MAN (FWYF1) at a height of 144 feet above sea level. Gusts over the coastal waters and immediate coast ranged from 40 knots at Miami Beach to 47 knots at Fowey Rocks. Unofficial observations of wind gusts of 48 knots (55 mph) were reported in Cutler Bay.||Damage was minor and mainly confined to a few downed power lines. Around 5,000 customers lost power in the three-county area of Palm Beach, Broward and Miami-Dade counties.   ||Rainfall amounts were rather light, ranging from a half-inch (0.5) to nearly 2 inches (1.94) 9 miles west of Sweetwater.||Lowest sea level pressures were in the 1003 to 1004 mb range.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front moving across north Florida with a narrow band of showers and thunderstorms spawned a tornado in the vicinity of Bunnell, Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A pilot traveling west on state road 100 from Interstate 95 to the Flagler county airport observed a narrow tornado touchdown along State Road 100 about 500 yards west of Interstate 95. The tornado was moving north and crossed State Road 100 damaging road signs and ripping apart palms. The vortex was observed on the ground for about 100 yards. Total time observed on the ground was around three minutes.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to severe thunderstorms developed along the sea breeze fronts and outflow boundaries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public reported penny size hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Lightning killed a man in Pinecrest on June 21st.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck and killed a 41-year-old man who was doing landscape work at a home at 10500 SW 62 Ave in Pinecrest. The man was under a tree when the lightning struck. ||It was not raining at the location of the lightning strike, although a thunderstorm was located about 2 to 3 miles to the north. The media reported this incident as dry lightning striking out of a clear sky, although the lightning bolt likely originated out of the side of the nearby storm.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to severe thunderstorms produced several reports of large hail and damaging winds across portions of the Florida Panhandle and Big Bend.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several trees were down along Springhill Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A light steering flow and unstable airmass fueled scattered severe storms across the forecast area along the sea breeze fronts and outflow boundaries. Lingering energy from a decaying MCV system across central Florida enhanced convection.

An area of low pressure formed in the eastern Gulf of Mexico in association with an upper level low. Shower and thunderstorm activity with this non-tropical system was limited but the bands of precipitation generally rotated north throughout the day in the Tampa Bay area and the motion curved to the northwest in the Cedar Key and The Villages area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The gust was measured at a home weather station.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to severe thunderstorms developed along the sea breeze fronts and outflow boundaries.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three inches of rain fell in 45 minutes in the Sugar Mill area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A surface pressure ridge axis extended across southern Florida and the west coast sea breeze front was most active from Tampa through Fort Myers.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a 16-year-old boy and his mother who were vacationing at the beach.  The bolt hit the boy in the chest, knocking him unconscious and shredding his shorts. The boy was taken to the hospital and died four days later on July 24.  The mother also received a jolt and complained of numbness in her feet and radiating pain reaching her chest.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Large hail and damaging winds affected the Tallahassee area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A few downed trees were reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cut-off upper level gulf low retrograded as an upper level ridge built over Florida from the Atlantic. However, upper level energy rotating around these features coupled with afternoon instability and sea breeze sparked scattered severe storms across the forecast area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The public measured a storm total rainfall of 4 inches from 630-845 pm local time near State Road 21 in Keystone Heights.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A quasi-stationary front just north of the Altamaha River, the sea breezes, surface based instability and upper level positive differential vorticity advection all merged to spawn scattered severe storms across our forecast area in the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The spotter reported that the funnel persisted for about 2 minutes before dissipating.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms over Northern Broward County produced lightning which killed    a 24-year-old man.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 24-year-old man was struck and killed by lightning while doing landscape work at a private residence in Parkland. The man was working with three other men when the lightning struck. It was not raining at the time, but there were dark storm clouds in the distance. None of the other men were injured.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level shortwave trough combined with afternoon heating helped to produce severe thunderstorms over Central Collier County during the late afternoon hours.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Three power poles down along with a path of trees downed north of Carnestown along State Road 29.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong ridge built north of the region with an elongated fetch area set up from near Bermuda to north Florida. A trough and eventually a low pressure center developed to the south of the region over the Bahamas and south Florida with a strong gradient flow between the two from September 29th through October 2nd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Heavy rainfall resulted in flooding of neighborhoods and portions of Race Track Road in northwestern St Johns County. At 18:00 EDT a storm spotter reported ankle deep water on her street in the Cimmaron subdivision off County Road 210. At 18:55 EDT a resident in Julington Creek Plantation reported two and a half feet of standing water flooding Roberts Road. Flooding was also reported on Highway 208 near the Whisper Ridge subdivision. Race Track Road did not reopen until 12:00 EDT on October 3rd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms affected parts of South Florida during the late afternoon and evening hours of July 2nd.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Wind gust estimated at 70 mph was reported by a trained spotter near the intersection of Rock Island Road and Margate Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong to severe thunderstorms produced several reports of wind damage and large hail across Leon and Wakulla counties.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Quarter size hail was reported.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Summertime pulse thunderstorms formed along an active sea breeze boundary between Sanford and Daytona Beach.
A supercell thunderstorm moved across eastern Broward County, producing an EF0 tornado and several reports of large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported near intersection of Commercial Boulevard and State Road 7 in Fort Lauderdale.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Pulse thunderstorms form over much of east central Florida and the coastal waters.
A band of thunderstorms moved from central Florida to the coast and Lake Okeechobee producing hail, strong winds and funnel clouds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Weather observers at the shuttle landing facility reported a funnel cloud to the west.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cut-off upper level gulf low retrograded as an upper level ridge built over Florida from the Atlantic. However, upper level energy rotating around these features coupled with afternoon instability and sea breeze sparked scattered severe storms across the forecast area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees were blown down on power lines near County Road 316 in Salt Springs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Mean layer southwest flow focused the sea breeze collision across the eastern Florida peninsula during the late afternoon. Isolated severe storms developed.
An isolated severe thunderstorm developed over Orange County and tracked eastward across northern Brevard County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A severe thunderstorm dropped two inch diameter hail on Kennedy Space Center. The hail severely damaged the foam on the external fuel tank of the Space Shuttle Atlantis. Due to the hail damage the shuttle was rolled back to the Assembly Building and the March 15th scheduled launch was postponed. There were estimates that 100 vehicles were damaged and a window was broken out of a guard shack. |

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe thunderstorms affected South Florida for the second day in a row due to the interaction of a weak surface trough across Central Florida and a mid to upper level trough over that same area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Reported at East 24th Street and 8th Avenue in Hialeah.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level short wave trough and surface cold front spawned severe weather across the forecast area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A fireman reported a funnel cloud about 3 miles north of the Marion county and Putnam county line, east of State Road 19.
